scenes.summary: The film begins with studio logos and the opening title sequence.
A young student named Barbara testifies in court against her teacher, Mr. Gettys, accusing him of molestation. Defense attorney Kevin Lomax listens as the prosecutor questions her.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Young Girl Testifies About Abuse in Tense Courtroom
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A young girl named Barbara takes the witness stand in a crowded courtroom to testify against her teacher. She tearfully recounts the harrowing details of how he sexually abused her after class. The prosecuting attorney emphasizes that the teacher also abused her friends, causing a stir among the onlookers. Following the intense testimony, defense attorney Kevin Lomax requests a short recess.

dialogue_highlights: He said he was testing to see if I had any special feelings, that he wanted to see what kind of feelings I had.
And after you told them what he had done to you, they told you what he had done to them! Isn't that true?
Any chance, Your Honor, for a short recess?

trivia.text: Another change from the novel was converting the book's lesbian client to the pedophile Lloyd Gettys, avoiding undertones of homophobia.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the trial of Mr. Gettys, who is accused of molesting his student Barbara.

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip takes place entirely within a courtroom, which was filmed at either the Bergen County Court House or a historic New York courthouse.

scenes.summary: During a recess, Kevin Lomax realizes his client is guilty and confronts him in the hallway. Lomax retreats to the bathroom to compose himself, ignoring a reporter's questions, before deciding to continue the trial.

content_desc.clip_title: Lawyer Confronts Client and Faces Pressure in Courthouse Restroom
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Following a recess in a tense courtroom, defense attorney Kevin Lomax angrily confronts his client, accusing him of lying and manipulation. Lomax retreats to the men's restroom to compose himself, removing his jacket and splashing water on his face. A smug reporter follows him inside, taunting Lomax about his impending first career loss and demanding a quote. Lomax dismisses the reporter and stares intensely at his own reflection in the mirror, grappling with the pressure.

dialogue_highlights: Lloyd, as your attorney, I'm advising you to keep the fuck away from me.
It was a nice run, Kev. Had to close out someday. Nobody wins them all.

trivia.text: Another change from the novel was converting the book's lesbian client to the pedophile Lloyd Gettys, avoiding undertones of homophobia.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene features Kevin Lomax confronting his client, Lloyd Gettys, whose pedophilic crimes are alluded to when Lomax mentions 'the kid' and 'the outrage'.

scenes.summary: Kevin Lomax aggressively cross-examines Barbara, using a note she wrote and a game she played to destroy her credibility. The judge adjourns the court for the day after an outburst from the gallery.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Intense Courtroom Cross-Examination Breaks Down Young Witness
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Defense attorney Kevin Lomax aggressively cross-examines a young girl named Barbara in a tense courtroom setting. He presents a mocking note she drew of her math teacher, Mr. Gettys, to undermine her credibility. Lomax then pressures her into admitting she fabricated abuse allegations to cover up a secret party game, causing her to break down in tears. The intense questioning provokes an angry outburst from a man in the gallery, forcing the judge to adjourn the session.

dialogue_highlights: A man's career... his reputation... his life is on the line! This is not a joke!
No. I didn't want to be the only one.
You goddamn low-life son of a bitch! You leave that girl alone!

trivia.text: Another change from the novel was converting the book's lesbian client to the pedophile Lloyd Gettys, avoiding undertones of homophobia.
trivia.match_reasoning: Kevin Lomax repeatedly mentions his client, Mr. Gettys, while aggressively cross-examining the student in this scene.

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The entire clip takes place during a trial in a courtroom, which was filmed at one of the locations mentioned in the fact.

scenes.summary: Kevin Lomax celebrates his courtroom victory with his wife Mary Ann and colleagues at a bar. A representative from a New York law firm approaches Kevin with a lucrative offer to help pick a jury.

content_desc.clip_title: Lawyer Celebrates Victory and Gets a Lucrative New York Offer
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Following a successful trial, defense attorney Kevin Lomax celebrates at a lively bar with his wife Mary Ann and his colleagues. Amidst the drinking and dancing, a mysterious man approaches Kevin with a proposition from a New York law firm. Kevin initially dismisses the offer as a prank orchestrated by his friends. However, his skepticism turns to shock when the man hands him an envelope containing an exorbitant lump sum payment.

dialogue_highlights: To the best damn trial lawyer in Alachua County.
We want you to come to New York, help us pick a jury.
A lump sum payment, regardless of the verdict, in the amount of... well, hey, take a look.

trivia.text: Another change from the novel was converting the book's lesbian client to the pedophile Lloyd Gettys, avoiding undertones of homophobia.
trivia.match_reasoning: Kevin's colleague explicitly asks him how it feels to get a man like "Gettys" acquitted, referencing the client mentioned in the fact.

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip begins with a courtroom scene, which were filmed at historic courthouses in New Jersey and New York.

scenes.summary: Kevin and Mary Ann visit Kevin's mother, Alice, at her church to say goodbye before their trip to New York. Alice warns Kevin about the evils of the city, quoting scripture, before they drive off.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Lomax Leaves Rural Church for New York City
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax attends a lively church service with his mother before stepping outside to discuss his upcoming trip to New York. His mother warns him about the city, quoting scripture and comparing it to Babylon. Kevin then joins his wife, Mary Ann, who is finishing a business call by their convertible, and the couple drives off together.

dialogue_highlights: Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great. It has become a dwelling place of demons.
Thou mighty city, in one hour hast thy mighty judgment come, and the light of a single lamp shall shine in thee no more.

trivia.text: The Gainesville church scenes were shot at a Gainesville church after Hackford persuaded the pastor and his members to participate, telling them that his story was about combating Satan.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the scene where Kevin visits his mother at her church, which was filmed at an actual church in Gainesville.

scenes.summary: During jury selection in a courtroom, Kevin advises the defense attorney on which jurors to dismiss. He demonstrates his keen ability to read people's hidden motives and backgrounds.

content_desc.clip_title: Jury Selection Disagreement in a New York Courtroom
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene opens with a sweeping aerial view of the New York City skyline before transitioning to a courtroom. A defense attorney questions a potential juror about his views on the banking industry. He then confers with his colleagues, where a younger lawyer strongly disagrees with his choices for the jury. The young lawyer insists on dismissing certain jurors based on his sharp, intuitive observations, ultimately taking control of the selection process.

dialogue_highlights: He may look like a brother with an attitude to you, but I see a man with a shotgun under his bed and woe betide the creature who steps into his garden.
Somebody hurt her, and she wants revenge.
I lose with your jury, you do the explaining.

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip takes place during a jury selection in a courtroom, and this fact details the real-world locations used to film the movie's court scenes.

scenes.summary: Kevin walks through the streets of New York City and a subway station. A time-lapse shows the city skyline transitioning from day to night and back to day.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Lomax Leaves Court and John Milton Observes
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene opens in a courtroom where Kevin Lomax sits with his legal team. He then exits a grand municipal building in Manhattan, walking down the steps into the busy street. John Milton is seen observing him from the shadows of an archway before descending into a subway station. The clip concludes with a rapid time-lapse of the New York City skyline transitioning through day and night.

setting.location: Courtroom, Manhattan Municipal Building, New York City streets, Chambers St Subway Station
setting.time_of_day: Day transitioning to Night and back to Day

dialogue_highlights: WE WOULD LIKE TO EXCUSE JURORS 3, 4, AND 6.

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip begins with a brief shot of a court scene, which were filmed at historic courthouses in New York and New Jersey.

scenes.summary: Kevin returns to his hotel room where Mary Ann is waiting. He reveals that the jury found his client not guilty in just 38 minutes, and they celebrate his victory.

content_desc.clip_title: Lawyer Surprises Wife with Court Victory
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A lawyer returns to his high-rise apartment and acts dejected, leading his wife to believe he lost a major case. She attempts to comfort him as he laments the quick jury deliberation. Suddenly, he breaks into a smile and reveals he actually won the case, prompting them both to jump up and celebrate enthusiastically.

characters: Kevin (lawyer, initially feigns sadness, then ecstatic)
Mary Ann (wife, supportive, then surprised and joyful)
dialogue_highlights: They deliberated for 38 minutes.
Not fucking guilty!
Oh, my God! You lying sack of shit!

scenes.summary: Leamon Heath gives Kevin a tour of the Milton Chadwick Waters law firm. Kevin observes the busy, international environment of the office.

content_desc.clip_title: Arrival at the New York Law Firm
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Two men in suits enter a luxurious, modern office building, arriving at the high-end law firm of Milton Chadwick Waters. One man gives the other a tour of the impressive facilities, highlighting the stunning view of the New York City skyline and explaining the firm's extensive international clientele. As they walk through the busy office, they pass various colleagues, including a woman intensely arguing in Italian on a headset.

setting.location: Milton Chadwick Waters law firm, New York City

dialogue_highlights: You can play it cool if you want, but when I first walked in, my jaw was on the floor.
So, am I good cop or bad cop?
We may not need a good cop.

trivia.text: Connie Nielsen, a Danish actress, was selected by Hackford for Christabella, who speaks several languages, with Hackford asserting that Nielsen already spoke multiple languages.
trivia.match_reasoning: Christabella is seen speaking Italian on a headset, demonstrating the character's multilingual abilities.

trivia.text: The offices were shot at the Continental Club in Manhattan, and at 180 Maiden Lane, although the water outside of Milton's office was added later by computer effects.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place in the law firm's offices, which were filmed at locations including 180 Maiden Lane.

trivia.text: In constructing the firm sets, Hackford and Rubeo consulted architects from Japan and Italy to craft an "ultra-modern" look, to display Milton's taste.
trivia.match_reasoning: The 'ultra-modern' firm sets designed for the film are prominently featured as Kevin is given a tour of the office.

scenes.summary: Kevin meets John Milton in his office. Milton questions Kevin about his background, his perfect winning streak, and his ability to handle pressure.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Lomax Meets John Milton
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax enters a spacious, imposing office and meets John Milton. They discuss Kevin's flawless legal record and his transition from prosecutor to defense attorney. After Kevin reveals a secret about his past cases, Milton leads him onto a high-rise balcony overlooking the city.

dialogue_highlights: That's our secret. Kill you with kindness.
I began the case with a clear conscience.
You don't really want to go back to Florida, though, do you?

trivia.text: Another change from the novel was converting the book's lesbian client to the pedophile Lloyd Gettys, avoiding undertones of homophobia.
trivia.match_reasoning: During their conversation, Milton explicitly asks Kevin about his work on 'the Gettys case'.

trivia.text: The offices were shot at the Continental Club in Manhattan, and at 180 Maiden Lane, although the water outside of Milton's office was added later by computer effects.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place in Milton's office, and at the end of the clip, Milton opens a set of doors to reveal a view that includes the water outside.

trivia.text: In constructing the firm sets, Hackford and Rubeo consulted architects from Japan and Italy to craft an "ultra-modern" look, to display Milton's taste.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene showcases the 'ultra-modern' design of Milton's office, featuring concrete walls, a minimalist fireplace, and sparse decor.

scenes.summary: Milton takes Kevin to the rooftop of the building, offering him a job. He continues to test Kevin's ambition and confidence while overlooking the city.

content_desc.clip_title: Milton Tests Kevin on a Skyscraper Rooftop
content_desc.summary_paragraph: John Milton brings Kevin Lomax to the edge of a towering New York City skyscraper rooftop, featuring a perilous infinity pool. As they look down at the dizzying drop, Milton probes into Kevin's family background and upbringing. The conversation shifts to business as Milton subtly offers Kevin a job, questioning his ability to handle intense pressure. Unfazed, Kevin confidently asks when they will discuss his compensation.

setting.location: Skyscraper rooftop with an infinity pool, New York City

key_actions: Looking down at the city streets
dialogue_highlights: Some people, you squeeze 'em, they focus. Others fold.
When do we talk about money?
Behold, I send you out a sheep amidst the wolves.

trivia.text: The offices were shot at the Continental Club in Manhattan, and at 180 Maiden Lane, although the water outside of Milton's office was added later by computer effects.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place on the rooftop outside Milton's office, featuring the large water element that was added via computer effects.

scenes.summary: Kevin, Mary Ann, and Leamon Heath arrive at a luxurious apartment building on Fifth Avenue in a limousine.

content_desc.clip_title: Arriving at the Carnegie Hill Apartment
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A black limousine navigates through the busy streets of New York City, passing yellow cabs and a school bus. It arrives at a luxurious apartment building on Fifth Avenue in Carnegie Hill. A young couple and their guide exit the vehicle and marvel at the towering structure. The guide details the neighborhood's amenities and explains the building's resident hierarchy, culminating with their boss in the penthouse tower.

dialogue_highlights: Follow the money all the way to the top.
And of course, Mr. Milton has the tower.

scenes.summary: Jackie Heath shows Kevin and Mary Ann a massive, empty apartment. Mary Ann is overjoyed by the space, and they decide to take the opportunity.

content_desc.clip_title: Touring the Classic 8 Apartment
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin and Mary Ann Lomax are shown a massive, luxurious New York apartment by their new neighbors, Jackie and Leamon Heath. Overwhelmed by the sheer size and prestige of the 'Classic 8', Mary Ann is ecstatic. Kevin offers to turn it down and go back to Florida if she wants, but she enthusiastically insists they stay, and they share a passionate kiss.

dialogue_highlights: It's what they call the 'Classic 8.'
I'm only getting behind this if you're on board.
Are you shittin' me?!

scenes.summary: John Milton introduces Kevin to the partners of the law firm in a large conference room.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Lomax Meets the Law Firm Partners
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene opens with a tender kiss between Kevin and his wife, Mary Ann. The setting then shifts abruptly to a sleek, high-rise corporate boardroom. John Milton formally introduces Kevin to the firm's senior partners, who each state their name and legal specialty. Kevin silently observes the intimidating group as Milton welcomes him to the team.

dialogue_highlights: I love you, too.
There it is, Kevin. The whole team. Welcome to Milton, Chadwick, and Waters.

trivia.text: Connie Nielsen, a Danish actress, was selected by Hackford for Christabella, who speaks several languages, with Hackford asserting that Nielsen already spoke multiple languages.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this clip as Christabella, introducing herself and her department to Kevin.

trivia.text: In constructing the firm sets, Hackford and Rubeo consulted architects from Japan and Italy to craft an "ultra-modern" look, to display Milton's taste.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place in the law firm's conference room, showcasing the ultra-modern set design crafted for the firm's offices.

scenes.summary: Milton shows Kevin his new office and introduces him to Pam Garrety. They discuss the Moyez case, and Kevin watches a video of an animal sacrifice.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Lomax Receives His First Case in New York
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax is shown his spacious new office overlooking New York City. He is introduced to Pam Garrety, a former D.A. employee who will assist him. Pam hands Kevin the file for the Moyez case, prompting him to watch a chaotic police raid video involving animal sacrifice. Kevin quickly realizes the seemingly unwinnable case is a test of his legal prowess.

setting.location: Law firm office, New York City

dialogue_highlights: It's a health code case, and it's a loser.
He wants to see if I'll plea-bargain. It's a test, right?
Isn't everything?
sound_design: Chaotic shouting and screaming on video

trivia.text: The offices were shot at the Continental Club in Manhattan, and at 180 Maiden Lane, although the water outside of Milton's office was added later by computer effects.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place within the law firm's offices, which were filmed at the Continental Club and 180 Maiden Lane, and features prominent views of the water outside the windows.

trivia.text: In constructing the firm sets, Hackford and Rubeo consulted architects from Japan and Italy to craft an "ultra-modern" look, to display Milton's taste.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene showcases the 'ultra-modern' design of the firm's sets, visible in both the opening boardroom shot and Kevin's new office.

scenes.summary: Mary Ann is looking at fabric samples with some women in the apartment. Kevin comes home and they kiss.

content_desc.clip_title: Negotiating Fabric Prices and a Surprise Visit
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A woman is examining expensive fabric samples with an interior designer, discussing the details of an 18th-century Italian silk damask. Her husband unexpectedly arrives and smoothly negotiates a lower price for the material. The couple then shares a tender moment, highlighting his prioritization of her needs despite his busy schedule.

dialogue_highlights: We'll take it... at 1,400.
I thought you said it was impossible. I thought you said it was important.

scenes.summary: Kevin goes to a rough neighborhood to find Phillipe Moyez. He enters a basement where a ritual is taking place and asks Moyez for the prosecutor's name.

content_desc.clip_title: Lawyer Meets Voodoo Practitioner in Dark Basement
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax arrives in a dilapidated neighborhood looking for Phillipe Moyez. After facing hostility from locals, a young boy leads him into a candlelit basement filled with voodoo altars and caged animals. Lomax introduces himself as Moyez's lawyer, and Moyez begins a ritual on an animal tongue to silence the opposing prosecutor.

dialogue_highlights: We have an investment in blood. Think of it as spiritual currency.
With this tongue, we create silence.

scenes.summary: Mary Ann is painting the apartment walls green. Jackie arrives and criticizes the color choice.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Disturbing Vision and a Blunt Paint Critique
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The clip begins with a sudden, disturbing close-up of a bloody, fleshy mass pinned to a table with nails. The scene then abruptly shifts to a domestic setting where a young woman is painting her apartment wall green. She turns to ask an elegantly dressed woman standing in the doorway for her opinion on the color. The woman in the doorway bluntly replies that the color does not suit the painter's complexion, leaving her looking taken aback.

characters: Young woman painting (hopeful, then taken aback)
dialogue_highlights: It's Arbor Green. What do you think?
Not with your complexion.

scenes.summary: Kevin is working late in his office, researching health codes. He hallucinates Mary Ann painting the wall of his office green.

content_desc.clip_title: Legal Research and Caribbean Green Paint
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene cuts between a woman painting her apartment wall and a lawyer working late in his office. A paralegal brings in a massive amount of law books for the lawyer to review, highlighting his heavy workload. A visual transition using the paint roller connects the overwhelming office environment back to the domestic setting, where the woman evaluates the new paint color.

key_actions: Reading legal text on a screen
Wiping the screen with a paint roller
dialogue_highlights: New York City Health Codes.
That's all of it? Not quite.
Caribbean green.

scenes.summary: Jackie is smoking in the apartment while Mary Ann paints the wall a different color, yellow. Jackie criticizes this color as well.

content_desc.clip_title: Unsettling Paint Color Advice
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Mary Ann is painting a room in her apartment while another woman stands nearby, offering unsolicited and critical advice on the paint colors. As Mary Ann tries different shades of green and yellow, the scene cuts rapidly, showing the other woman in various outfits and positions around the room. This surreal progression creates a disorienting and unsettling atmosphere, leaving Mary Ann appearing increasingly confused and frustrated by the passive-aggressive commentary.

characters: Mary Ann (blonde woman, confused and frustrated)
dialogue_highlights: Too institutional.
Mary Ann, I'm just trying to help. I mean, it's your walls. Do what you want.
sound_design: Sound of paint roller on wall

scenes.summary: Kevin is still working late, eating fried chicken. He imagines Mary Ann eating chicken in his office before Pam enters and tells him to go home.

content_desc.clip_title: Late Night Studying and Wallpaper Tearing
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A young lawyer works late into the night, surrounded by open law books and case files in a messy office. An older woman stops by to remind him not to overwork himself on a simple case before leaving. Meanwhile, in a separate location, a woman casually eats fried chicken while inspecting and then aggressively tearing down wallpaper in an apartment.

dialogue_highlights: I mean, it's your walls. Do what you want.
Remember, it's only a health code case. Good night.

scenes.summary: Mary Ann and Jackie are drinking champagne. Mary Ann expresses feeling lost without a job, and Jackie tells her she has three choices in life.

content_desc.clip_title: The Holy Trinity of Choices
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Two women share a drink in a luxurious living room, discussing the challenges and isolation of their affluent lifestyles. The blonde woman expresses feeling aimless and adrift without a job, while her friend cynically explains the distant reality of her own marriage. The friend concludes by outlining the three stark choices available to women in their position: work, play, or breed.

dialogue_highlights: God, I just feel like I'm floating around in that place.
If I want to see Leamon... I make an appointment.
Look, you got 3 choices, the holy trinity: you can work... you can play... or you can breed.

scenes.summary: In the courtroom, Kevin defends Moyez's actions as religious freedom. The prosecutor, Merto, suddenly suffers a violent coughing fit, leading the judge to dismiss the case.

content_desc.clip_title: Courtroom Defense of Religious Animal Sacrifice
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A defense attorney delivers a passionate courtroom argument defending his client's right to perform animal sacrifice as a religious practice. He compares the act to other accepted religious rituals to highlight the city's hypocrisy. As he speaks, the opposing counsel is suddenly overcome by a severe and unexplained coughing fit. The defense attorney confidently moves for a dismissal based on constitutional protections.

key_actions: Presenting a piece of meat as evidence
dialogue_highlights: Phillipe Moyez killed a goat, and he did it while observing his constitutionally protected religious beliefs.
The city was clearly less concerned with the care and feeding of the animals than in the manner with which they were slaughtered.

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The entire clip takes place during a court scene, which were filmed at the Bergen County Court House and historic courthouses in New York.

scenes.summary: Outside the courtroom, Kevin is congratulated by Pam, Moyez, and Milton on his victory.

content_desc.clip_title: Courtroom Victory and Congratulations
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin exits a courtroom after a successful case, accompanied by colleagues and a client. He is greeted by John Milton, who claps and congratulates him enthusiastically. Milton also greets the others, projecting a charismatic and commanding presence.

dialogue_highlights: Bravo, Kevin! Bravo!
So, Phillipe, satisfied?
Kevin, what can I say?

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The characters are seen exiting a courtroom into a courthouse hallway, locations which were filmed at New Jersey's Bergen County Court House and historic New York courthouses.

scenes.summary: Milton and Kevin walk through Chinatown. Milton reveals Moyez's wealth and advises Kevin on the power of being underestimated.

content_desc.clip_title: Milton Mentors Kevin on the Streets of Chinatown
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Milton and Kevin walk through a bustling Chinatown street, discussing their recent legal victory and the nature of their wealthy clients. Milton imparts cynical wisdom to Kevin, advising him to remain underestimated and unassuming to maintain a strategic advantage. He uses a crude but vivid metaphor to explain his own success, emphasizing the power of surprise. The scene ends with Milton enthusiastically looking for a famous tic-tac-toe playing chicken before leading Kevin to the subway.

dialogue_highlights: I'm the hand up Mona Lisa's skirt. I'm a surprise, Kevin. They don't see me comin'.
Don't ever let them see you coming. That's the gaffe, my friend. You got to keep yourself small, innocuous, and be the little guy.

scenes.summary: Kevin returns to the apartment, which is now painted yellow. Mary Ann is getting ready for a party, and they share a moment in the elevator.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin and Mary Ann Prepare for a High-Society Party
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin arrives at his apartment to find construction underway and his wife, Mary Ann, getting ready for an important social event. Mary Ann expresses her frustration over Kevin's long working hours, sarcastically suggesting she build a law library instead of a nursery. Later, in the elevator, a nervous Mary Ann makes Kevin promise not to leave her side at the intimidating gathering.

dialogue_highlights: It was gonna be a nursery, but then I remembered you have to be home long enough to knock me up, so now I'm thinking of building a law library.
Promise me you won't leave me alone in there.

scenes.summary: Kevin and Mary Ann arrive at Milton's party and mingle with the guests. Milton gives Mary Ann advice on her hair, suggesting she wear it down. Kevin is pulled away for business.

content_desc.clip_title: John Milton Charms Mary Ann at a High-Society Party
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin and Mary Ann Lomax arrive at a lavish high-society party hosted by Eddie Barzoon. While Kevin networks with powerful politicians and businessmen, Mary Ann is approached by John Milton. Milton seductively advises Mary Ann to change her hairstyle, manipulating her with poetic compliments about her shoulders and neck.

dialogue_highlights: A woman's shoulders are the front lines of her mystique.
The worst vice is advice.
Already swimming with the sharks. Careful, huh?

scenes.summary: Kevin steps out onto the balcony and meets Christabella. They have a flirtatious conversation about the city and her living arrangements.

pg0WhF.name: Illegal Drugs, Tobacco, eCigarettes, Vaping, Alcohol
pg0WhF.tier2: Illegal Drugs, Tobacco, eCigarettes, Vaping, Alcohol

content_desc.clip_title: Flirtatious Encounter on a City Terrace
content_desc.summary_paragraph: At a sophisticated evening party, Kevin Lomax steps out onto a high-rise terrace where he engages in a flirtatious and suggestive conversation with Christabella. Meanwhile, his wife Mary Ann searches for him inside among the party guests. The scene builds tension as Christabella tests Kevin's boundaries with double entendres before John Milton interrupts them.

dialogue_highlights: Is your wife a jealous woman, Mr. Lomax?
Bet you like to be on top, don't you? ... Of the situation.
I see the future of this law firm.

trivia.text: Connie Nielsen, a Danish actress, was selected by Hackford for Christabella, who speaks several languages, with Hackford asserting that Nielsen already spoke multiple languages.
trivia.match_reasoning: Connie Nielsen appears in this scene as Christabella, who demonstrates her multilingual abilities by speaking Italian to Kevin.

scenes.summary: Milton interrupts Kevin and Christabella, summoning Kevin to his office. They discuss a new, high-profile murder case involving Alexander Cullen. Cullen arrives and is hesitant to hire Kevin, but Milton convinces him. Meanwhile, Mary Ann is left alone at the party.

content_desc.clip_title: Milton Assigns Kevin a High-Profile Murder Case
content_desc.summary_paragraph: During a party, John Milton pulls Kevin Lomax aside to handle an urgent situation. They gather in Milton's imposing penthouse office with Eddie Barzoon and Leamon Heath. Milton reveals that their wealthy client, Alex Cullen, has been charged with a triple murder and insists that Kevin lead the defense. Despite Eddie's strong objections regarding Kevin's experience, Milton stands firm, and Kevin steps up to take the call.

dialogue_highlights: Alex Cullen has just been charged with murdering his wife, her stepson, and a maid.
I take Kevin, and I don't look back.
You gonna fight for this thing or not?

trivia.text: Connie Nielsen, a Danish actress, was selected by Hackford for Christabella, who speaks several languages, with Hackford asserting that Nielsen already spoke multiple languages.
trivia.match_reasoning: Christabella is seen speaking Italian to Kevin at the beginning of the clip, reflecting the fact that the character speaks several languages.

trivia.text: Shots of ballerinas moving in water were used as a basis for Milton's animated sculpture.
trivia.match_reasoning: Milton's animated sculpture is prominently featured on the wall of his office and is shown moving in a close-up.

trivia.text: Williams said that he filmed the people for the statue effect, and that they were naked and placed in a tank next to a blue screen.
trivia.match_reasoning: This fact details the visual effects process used to create the animated statue seen on the wall of Milton's office.

trivia.text: It took three months to film the people and add the computer effects, at a cost of $2 million, which was 40% of the overall budget for special effects.
trivia.match_reasoning: This fact provides the production details for the computer effects used to animate the sculpture visible in Milton's office.

scenes.summary: Kevin returns home to find Mary Ann angry that he left her alone at the party for hours. They argue, and she tells him to sleep on the couch.

pg0WhF.name: Illegal Drugs, Tobacco, eCigarettes, Vaping, Alcohol
pg0WhF.tier2: Illegal Drugs, Tobacco, eCigarettes, Vaping, Alcohol

content_desc.clip_title: Husband and Wife Argue Over Abandonment
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A man returns to his dark apartment to find his wife drinking by the window. She angrily confronts him for abandoning her at a party for three hours. He defends his absence by citing a major career opportunity. Unmoved, she walks away and tells him to sleep on the couch.

dialogue_highlights: YOU LEFT ME THERE. I HAD A FUCKIN' PANIC ATTACK
I'm about to catch a triple homicide defendant
AND YOU CAN GO SLEEP ON THE FUCKIN' COUCH!

scenes.summary: Cullen, Milton, Kevin, and Eddie meet at a construction site to discuss Cullen's defense strategy. Cullen is panicked about the media and the evidence against him. Kevin asserts his authority and demands Cullen's trust.

content_desc.clip_title: High-Stakes Legal Pitch on a Skyscraper Construction Site
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Wealthy real estate developer Alex Cullen is confronted by a legal team on the top floor of an unfinished skyscraper. Panicked about being accused of a triple murder and hounded by the press, he is hesitant to trust anyone. John Milton and Kevin Lomax pressure him to hire Lomax immediately to handle the PR and legal fallout. Cullen eventually agrees to a private meeting with Lomax, demanding he live up to his reputation.

dialogue_highlights: Jury selection started this morning at every breakfast table in the city.
You better be every fuckin' inch as good as he says you are!

scenes.summary: Mary Ann is shopping with Jackie and other wealthy women. They are drinking wine and trying on expensive clothes. Jackie shows off her new breast implants to Mary Ann, who seems uncomfortable.

content_desc.clip_title: Women Gossip in Boutique Before a Shocking Demonic Reveal
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Mary Ann enters a high-end clothing boutique with wealthy acquaintances who drink wine and try on expensive outfits. The women gossip about their absent husbands, spending money, and the results of recent plastic surgery. One woman insists Mary Ann feel her surgically enhanced breasts to prove how real they seem. When the woman turns to change her top, a terrifying demonic face briefly manifests on her back, leaving Mary Ann deeply shocked and disturbed.

dialogue_highlights: If you're never going to see your husband, you might as well have a relationship with his money.
Feel 'em. That's the ultimate test.
Dr. Robert, I'm telling you. He's the miracle man.

scenes.summary: Mary Ann is upset and tells Kevin she hates her new life and friends. She feels lonely and disconnected from him. They argue, but then she asks him to make love to her. During sex, Mary Ann becomes distressed and asks him to stop.

content_desc.clip_title: Heated Argument Transitions into Passionate Intimacy in Apartment
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A woman named Mary Ann is highly distressed, arguing with her husband Kevin about feeling lonely and overwhelmed in their new, empty apartment. Kevin attempts to comfort her, and the tension shifts when he suggests they have a baby. The couple begins kissing passionately, undressing, and moving to the floor to make love. However, during their intimate moment, Mary Ann suddenly stops him, feeling a deep emotional disconnect and stating that he isn't truly there with her.

dialogue_highlights: I am so lonely. I miss you so much.
Let's make a baby.
Where are you? Right here. No. No, you're not.

scenes.summary: Kevin is questioning Alexander Cullen about the murders. Cullen demonstrates how he found the gun and reveals he still has it. Kevin demands the gun, and Cullen reluctantly gives it to him.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Lomax Confronts Cullen Over a Hidden Handgun
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Defense attorney Kevin Lomax questions his wealthy client, Alexander Cullen, in his lavish New York penthouse to reconstruct the events of a triple homicide. Tensions rise as Cullen becomes frustrated with the interrogation and suddenly pulls out a handgun to demonstrate his actions. Shocked that a murder suspect is carrying a weapon, Lomax demands he hand it over, citing the danger it poses to their case. Cullen reluctantly surrenders the gun, claiming he needs it for protection due to death threats.

dialogue_highlights: You're charged with a triple homicide, you're walking around with a damn gun?
I have had 9 death threats.
This is a deal-breaker, Alex.

trivia.text: Donald Trump's penthouse in Trump Tower, Fifth Avenue, was lent to the production for Alexander Cullen's residence.
trivia.match_reasoning: The lavish, gold-decorated penthouse seen in this scene is Donald Trump's penthouse in Trump Tower, which served as the filming location for Alexander Cullen's residence.

scenes.summary: Kevin and his mother arrive at his apartment building, swarmed by reporters. Inside, they meet John Milton and his companions. Alice seems uneasy around Milton. Kevin and Mary Ann argue briefly before bed.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin's Mother Meets John Milton in New York
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax and his wife Mary Ann arrive at their New York apartment building with Kevin's mother, Alice, amidst a swarm of reporters. Inside, they unexpectedly run into Kevin's boss, John Milton, accompanied by two beautiful women. Milton introduces himself to Alice, creating an awkward and tense atmosphere in the elevator. Later, Mary Ann expresses jealousy over the women Milton was with, suspecting Kevin's late nights at work.

dialogue_highlights: Welcome to Babylon, Ma.
You've done one hell of a job bringing him up, Mrs. Lomax. And I'm sure... it wasn't easy.
That what you're doing when you're working late?

scenes.summary: The next morning, Kevin finds his mother packing to leave. She expresses concern for Mary Ann's well-being and dislikes New York. Kevin gets angry and leaves for work.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Argues With His Mother About Temptation
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin reads a damaging headline about his current case in the morning newspaper and rushes to get ready for work. He is intercepted by his mother, who announces she is leaving New York because she feels uneasy and is deeply concerned about his wife Mary Ann's well-being. Kevin defensively insists that New York is their home now and refuses to return to Florida. As he storms out, his mother quotes scripture warning him about the path to temptation, which he dismisses.

dialogue_highlights: I just got my teeth kicked in on the front page, so I gotta run.
This place is not good for her, Kevin.
Wide is the gate and broad is the way that leadeth to temptation.

scenes.summary: Kevin reviews crime scene photos and discusses the babysitter's testimony with Pam.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Lomax Reviews Crime Photos and Reassures a Witness
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax grimly reviews graphic crime scene photographs of a murdered family in a dimly lit office. The scene shifts to a nighttime view of the New York City skyline before moving to a law firm hallway. Lomax and his team walk with a witness, Melissa, who expresses concern about her babysitter testifying. Melissa firmly states her belief in the defendant's innocence before departing.

dialogue_highlights: Mr. Cullen didn't kill those people. I know him. He's a difficult man, but he couldn't kill anyone.
That's why we're all working so hard.

scenes.summary: Kevin discovers Barzoon shredding documents late at night before Milton arrives and invites him to a boxing match.

content_desc.clip_title: Late Night Office Document Cover-Up
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax walks into the law firm late at night to find his colleagues frantically shredding mountains of documents. Eddie Barzoon nervously tries to play it off as routine 'housekeeping' and warns Kevin to keep quiet about what he saw. The tense atmosphere is broken when John Milton suddenly appears and invites Kevin out to a boxing match.

dialogue_highlights: If anyone should ask, you never saw this.
Grab your coat, Mr. Lomax. The night is young.

scenes.summary: Milton and Kevin ride the subway, where Milton intimidates two thugs speaking Spanish.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Subway Confrontation and Mind Reading
content_desc.summary_paragraph: While discussing business on a crowded subway train, Kevin Lomax and John Milton are confronted by two aggressive men. When one of the men pulls a box cutter, Milton calmly diffuses the situation by speaking to him in Spanish and revealing intimate, impossible-to-know details about his wife's current infidelity. Spooked by Milton's supernatural knowledge, the men quickly exit the train, leaving Kevin bewildered while Milton laughs off the encounter.

dialogue_highlights: I ain't asking you, motherfucker. I'm telling you. Get the fuck out of my car.
What did you say to him?
I told him that if he didn't leave us alone, you were gonna kick his ass...

scenes.summary: Milton and Kevin watch a boxing match from ringside, and Milton introduces Kevin to Don King.

content_desc.clip_title: Ringside Introductions at a Boxing Match
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax and John Milton attend a high-stakes boxing match at Madison Square Garden. As the fight intensifies, one boxer delivers a knockout blow, sending the crowd into a frenzy. Following the match, Milton enthusiastically introduces Kevin to legendary boxing promoter Don King.

dialogue_highlights: I want you to meet my new heavyweight here. Kevin Lomax.

scenes.summary: At a flamenco club, Kevin steps away to argue with Mary Ann on the phone about his late hours.

pg0WhF.name: Illegal Drugs, Tobacco, eCigarettes, Vaping, Alcohol
pg0WhF.tier2: Illegal Drugs, Tobacco, eCigarettes, Vaping, Alcohol

content_desc.clip_title: Flamenco Club and Marital Tension
content_desc.summary_paragraph: While Milton and his guests enjoy a lively flamenco performance at a club, Kevin steps away to take a tense phone call from his wife, Mary Ann. Kevin defends his boss and his new lifestyle, smoking a cigarette despite having quit. He then returns to the table, where Milton introduces him to two women, Babs and Tiffany, amidst the energetic atmosphere.

setting.location: Flamenco club and restaurant kitchen hallway

dialogue_highlights: This guy pays our bills. The food, the heat, the rent on that enormous fuckin' apartment we live in.
One cigarette after 7 months is not-- That's exactly what I'm saying.

scenes.summary: Mary Ann wakes up to strange noises, grabs a knife, and hallucinates a demonic toddler playing with her ovaries before Kevin arrives to comfort her.

content_desc.clip_title: Mary Ann's Terrifying Nightmare and Devastating Confession
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Mary Ann wakes up alone in her dark apartment and hears strange scurrying noises. Armed with a kitchen knife, she investigates and discovers a demonic toddler playing with a bloody mass of tissue. She wakes up screaming, realizing it was a nightmare, but tearfully reveals to her husband Kevin that a doctor confirmed she is infertile. Despite her severe emotional distress and pleas for him to listen, Kevin prioritizes a sudden work phone call over comforting her.

key_actions: Investigating strange noises in the dark
Taking a work call during a personal crisis
dialogue_highlights: They took my ovaries, Kevin. I told you! Right here!
Kevin, I can't have children.
It's not a dream if it's true.

scenes.summary: Kevin confronts Alexander Cullen outside the Tice Institute, and Cullen admits he was sleeping with his assistant on the night of the murder.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Lawyer Confronts Client About Murder Motive and Infidelity
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A defense attorney confronts his wealthy client on a snowy street, revealing he knows about the client's infidelity, which provides a financial motive for his wife's murder. The client confesses to sleeping with his assistant on the night of the murder. Their tense conversation is abruptly interrupted when the client's supervised visitation with his stepdaughter is cut short due to the lawyer's intrusion.

dialogue_highlights: You fuck around, she gets rich. That's a motive, Alex.
I was boning my assistant the night my wife got shot.
Juries want honesty. Fuck with that at your peril.

scenes.summary: Kevin and Milton walk down the street discussing the Cullen case. Milton suggests Kevin drop the case to care for his sick wife, but Kevin insists on finishing it quickly to secure a win.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Refuses to Drop the Case for His Wife
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin and John walk through the city streets while Kevin discusses his wife's medication on the phone. John unexpectedly suggests that Kevin step down from the high-profile Cullen case to care for her. Kevin refuses, confessing his dark fear that he would resent his wife if he sacrificed his winning case for her recovery.

dialogue_highlights: I quit the case, she gets better... and I hate her for it.
I'm taking you off the case.
You mean the possibility of leaving this case has never even entered your mind?

scenes.summary: In the courtroom, the prosecutor delivers his opening statement. Kevin then gives his opening statement, telling the jury that while Cullen is an unlikable person, he has an alibi for the murders.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Defense Attorney's Unconventional Opening Statement
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a tense courtroom, the prosecution concludes its opening statement by accusing Alexander Cullen of a cold-blooded triple murder. Defense attorney Kevin Lomax then takes the floor, surprising the jury by openly admitting his client is a despicable and unlikable man. However, Lomax pivots his argument, insisting that Cullen's poor character is irrelevant because he has a solid alibi. He challenges the jury to separate their personal distaste for the defendant from the factual evidence of the murder charge.

dialogue_highlights: I don't like Alexander Cullen. I don't think he's a nice person.
But this isn't a popularity contest. It's a murder trial.
Is not liking this man reason enough to convict him of murder?

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: This scene takes place in a courtroom, which was filmed at either the Bergen County Court House or a historic New York courthouse.

scenes.summary: Cullen angrily confronts Kevin in a stairwell about his opening statement. Kevin explains it was a tactic to gain the jury's trust and emphasize Cullen's alibi.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Tense Office Confrontation and Strategic Manipulation
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A younger man aggressively shoves an older colleague against an office wall, demanding an explanation for his recent behavior. The older man reveals a calculated strategy to create an alibi for the younger man by making him appear unlikable and distracted by an affair. Realizing the manipulation was intentional to appear spontaneous, the younger man backs down as the older man calmly adjusts his tie.

dialogue_highlights: I'M GONNA BUST MY ASS TO MAKE SURE THEY HATE YOU, BECAUSE AS LONG AS YOU'RE OUT BONING MELISSA, YOU'RE NOT HOME KILLING YOUR WIFE!
WHY DIDN'T YOU SAY SOMETHING BEFORE? THEN IT DOESN'T LOOK SPONTANEOUS.

scenes.summary: On the street, Eddie Barzoon aggressively confronts Kevin, accusing him of trying to steal his job after seeing Kevin's name on the firm's charter. Eddie threatens Kevin before jogging away.

content_desc.clip_title: Eddie Confronts Kevin About His Secret Partnership
content_desc.summary_paragraph: While walking down a busy city street, Kevin is intercepted by an angry Eddie. Eddie accuses Kevin of going behind his back to get his name added to the firm's charter as a partner. Kevin denies any knowledge of this and pushes back against Eddie's aggressive tone. In response, Eddie threatens to expose the firm's secrets to an ongoing investigation if he is challenged again.

dialogue_highlights: How the fuck does your name get in the firm's charter?
You want my job, take me head on.
Tell your mentor the next time Weaver calls me, maybe I'll just pick up the phone.

scenes.summary: Kevin tells Milton about Eddie's paranoia. Milton delivers a monologue about greed and ego, while intercut scenes show Eddie being chased and brutally murdered by homeless men in Central Park.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Milton's Monologue on Greed and Eddie Barzoon's Demise
content_desc.summary_paragraph: As Milton delivers a cynical and apocalyptic monologue about human greed and the destructive nature of modern society, his associate Eddie Barzoon is shown jogging through Central Park. Eddie's run turns into a nightmare as he is pursued and brutally beaten to death by a group of vagrants who take on a demonic appearance. Meanwhile, Kevin listens intently to Milton's speech, and Mary Ann watches in horror from her window, seemingly sensing the unfolding tragedy.

setting.location: Milton's office and Central Park, New York City

dialogue_highlights: YOU SHARPEN THE HUMAN APPETITE TO THE POINT WHERE IT CAN SPLIT ATOMS WITH ITS DESIRE.
WE GOT A RUNAWAY TRAIN, BOY. WE GOT A BILLION EDDIE BARZOONS ALL JOGGING INTO THE FUTURE.
MAYBE GOD THREW THE DICE ONCE TOO OFTEN. MAYBE HE LET US ALL DOWN.

trivia.text: Hackford wanted suggestions that Milton does not kill Barzoon as he defied his muggers, or United States Attorney Weaver, who arrogantly did not watch for vehicles before stepping onto the road.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip intercuts Milton's monologue with scenes of Eddie Barzoon being chased and ultimately killed after defying his muggers in the park.

trivia.text: Shots of ballerinas moving in water were used as a basis for Milton's animated sculpture.
trivia.match_reasoning: Milton's animated sculpture is prominently visible on the wall behind his desk throughout the scene.

trivia.text: Williams said that he filmed the people for the statue effect, and that they were naked and placed in a tank next to a blue screen.
trivia.match_reasoning: The statue effect, which features moving figures, is clearly visible on the wall in Milton's office.

trivia.text: It took three months to film the people and add the computer effects, at a cost of $2 million, which was 40% of the overall budget for special effects.
trivia.match_reasoning: The computer effects used to animate the sculpture on Milton's wall are visible during his monologue.

scenes.summary: Kevin aggressively preps Melissa Black for her testimony, acting as a ruthless prosecutor. He pushes her with vulgar questions until she breaks character and yells at him.

content_desc.clip_title: Lawyer Aggressively Preps Witness for Trial Testimony
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin, a defense attorney, intensely prepares a witness named Ms. Black for her upcoming trial testimony in a high-rise office. He paces the room, firing rapid and increasingly personal questions at her to test her composure under pressure. When he asks a highly specific and vulgar question about the defendant's anatomy, she loses her temper and lashes out, demonstrating exactly how the prosecution might break her.

dialogue_highlights: He's gonna come out with questions you never thought of. Just to screw you up.
I've had enough of your fucking game! Why don't you take your questions and shove 'em up your ass?

scenes.summary: Kevin arrives at the law firm to find his colleagues in tears. Pam informs him that Eddie Barzoon was killed in the park, and Milton tells Kevin he needs to get to court.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Learns of Eddie Barzoon's Murder
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin enters a bustling office hallway where colleagues are visibly upset and whispering. A distraught woman approaches him and breaks the news that their colleague, Eddie Barzoon, was murdered in the park the previous night. While another coworker mentions the suspects have been caught, an older assistant interrupts to remind Kevin of his impending court appearance. In the background, John Milton silently observes the unfolding scene.

dialogue_highlights: It's Eddie Barzoon. He's dead. He was killed.
At least they got the scumbags who did it.
Kevin, I'm sorry. You're due in court in 30 minutes.

trivia.text: Hackford wanted suggestions that Milton does not kill Barzoon as he defied his muggers, or United States Attorney Weaver, who arrogantly did not watch for vehicles before stepping onto the road.
trivia.match_reasoning: Pam informs Kevin that Barzoon was killed in the park by "two old homeless crazies" while running, which aligns with the fact about Barzoon defying his muggers.

trivia.text: The offices were shot at the Continental Club in Manhattan, and at 180 Maiden Lane, although the water outside of Milton's office was added later by computer effects.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene takes place in the law firm's offices, which were filmed at the Continental Club and 180 Maiden Lane.

trivia.text: In constructing the firm sets, Hackford and Rubeo consulted architects from Japan and Italy to craft an "ultra-modern" look, to display Milton's taste.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene showcases the "ultra-modern" design of the firm's office sets as Kevin walks through the hallways.

scenes.summary: On the subway, Milton advises Kevin to focus on the trial. Kevin expresses doubt about putting Melissa on the stand, believing Cullen set him up, but Milton tells him to follow his gut.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Intense Subway Conversation About a Murder Case
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Two men are having a tense conversation on a moving subway train. The younger man, Kevin, is visibly stressed, rubbing his face and pacing the aisle, while the older man remains seated and calm. They discuss a legal case involving a woman named Melissa and a man named Cullen, whom Kevin believes is guilty of murder and has set him up. The older man advises Kevin to go with his gut, reminding him about the nature of pressure and suggesting that it might simply be his time to lose.

dialogue_highlights: He killed those people.
Maybe it's your time to lose.
We talked about this, remember? Pressure.

scenes.summary: Kevin enters the courtroom and is called upon by the judge to present his case. After a long, tense hesitation, he decides to call Melissa Black to the stand.

content_desc.clip_title: Courtroom Trial Begins with Hesitant Lawyer
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a crowded courtroom, the judge enters and takes his seat, prompting the trial to begin. Defense attorney Kevin Lomax is called upon to present his case but hesitates, appearing deeply conflicted. Under the watchful eye of John Milton in the gallery, Lomax finally breaks the silence and calls his first witness, Melissa Black, to the stand.

key_actions: Calling a witness to the stand
dialogue_highlights: All right, Mr. Lomax. You may present your case.
I call Melissa Black.

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip takes place entirely within a courtroom, which was filmed at either the Bergen County Court House or a historic courthouse in New York.

scenes.summary: Kevin and Cullen walk through a crowd of reporters after the trial. Cullen maintains his innocence.

content_desc.clip_title: Courtroom Oath and Media Frenzy
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A woman is sworn in as a witness in a tense courtroom, promising to tell the truth with her hand on a Holy Bible. Kevin Lomax and John Milton watch the proceedings closely from the gallery. Following the session, the legal team and their client exit into a chaotic hallway, swarmed by reporters and flashing cameras as the client declares his innocence.

dialogue_highlights: DO YOU SOLEMNLY SWEAR TO TELL THE TRUTH, THE WHOLE TRUTH AND NOTHING BUT THE TRUTH, SO HELP YOU, GOD?
I DO.
I HAVE MAINTAINED MY INNOCENCE ALL ALONG.

trivia.text: For court scenes, New Jersey's Bergen County Court House was employed for production, as were historic courthouses in New York.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip begins with a scene taking place inside a courtroom, which was filmed at either the Bergen County Court House or a historic New York courthouse.

scenes.summary: Kevin asks a doorman about his wife's whereabouts and is told she went to a church. He runs off to find her.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Lomax Rushes to Find His Wife
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A man in a suit hurriedly exits a yellow taxi and is approached by a doorman. The doorman informs him that a woman, presumably his wife, did not look well and went to the Church of the Heavenly Hope. The man immediately sprints down the city sidewalk in a panic.

setting.location: City street sidewalk, New York City

dialogue_highlights: She didn't look well, sir.
Where is she?
Church of the Heavenly Hope.

trivia.text: The interior of New York City's Church of the Heavenly Rest was used for the scene in which Theron's character says that Milton raped her.
trivia.match_reasoning: The doorman explicitly tells Kevin that his wife went to the Church of the Heavenly Rest, the real-life location discussed in this fact.

scenes.summary: Kevin finds Mary Ann in a church, wrapped in a blanket. She tells him that Milton raped her and reveals she has cut herself.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Church Confession of Milton's Attack
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin finds his traumatized wife huddled in a church pew wrapped in a blanket. She tearfully confesses that Milton assaulted her earlier that afternoon. Kevin angrily dismisses her claim, stating Milton was with him in court all day. Desperate to be believed, she drops her blanket to reveal severe scratches on her body.

dialogue_highlights: He let himself in. You need to know that.
Today he was in court in my presence all afternoon!
Kevin, I'm not crazy. You have to believe me.

trivia.text: The interior of New York City's Church of the Heavenly Rest was used for the scene in which Theron's character says that Milton raped her.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the scene inside a church where Mary Ann (Theron) tells Kevin that Milton raped her.

scenes.summary: Kevin signs papers to commit Mary Ann to a psychiatric ward. He visits her as she is being wheeled away, and she begs him not to do it.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Comforts a Distressed Mary Ann Before Hospitalization
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Mary Ann is found in a church, highly distressed and covered in scratches, claiming someone else inflicted the wounds. Kevin comforts her and subsequently admits her to a psychiatric hospital. While lying in a hospital bed, Mary Ann tearfully tells Kevin that their suffering is the result of the 'blood money' he earns from defending guilty clients. As she is wheeled away by orderlies, she begs Kevin to take her home, insisting she is not crazy.

dialogue_highlights: HE DID THIS TO ME!
IT'S THE MONEY. BLOOD MONEY, KEVIN. WE JUST DRANK IT DOWN, BOTH OF US.
PLEASE TAKE ME HOME. PLEASE DON'T DO THIS. I'M NOT CRAZY.

trivia.text: The interior of New York City's Church of the Heavenly Rest was used for the scene in which Theron's character says that Milton raped her.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip begins with the scene inside a church where Theron's character, found naked and covered in scratches, claims she was attacked.

scenes.summary: Kevin attends Eddie Barzoon's funeral. Milton and his associates are also there, and Kevin looks at them with suspicion.

content_desc.clip_title: Eddie Barzoon's Funeral
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax and his colleagues attend the funeral of Eddie Barzoon in a grand, ornate church. While the priest delivers a solemn eulogy praising the deceased, the attendees hypocritically engage in superficial gossip and whisper about Mary Ann's deteriorating mental state. As the service continues, John Milton makes a late, dramatic entrance into the sanctuary, quietly observing the congregation.

dialogue_highlights: We are here today to remember Edward Barzoon.
Doesn't Diana look fabulous?
She's falling apart. Hallucinations.

trivia.text: The outside of Central Presbyterian Church was photographed for Barzoon's funeral, while Pacino was inside the Manhattan Church of the Most Holy Redeemer for the holy water sequence.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the interior of the church during Barzoon's funeral where Pacino's character dips his fingers into the holy water, and concludes with an exterior shot of the church.

scenes.summary: Mitch Weaver from the Justice Department approaches Kevin outside the funeral, warning him about Milton's firm. Weaver is then hit by a car and killed.

XtODT3.name: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations
XtODT3.tier2: Crime & Harmful Acts to Individuals, Society & Human Right Violations

content_desc.clip_title: Justice Department Agent Killed by Car
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax is confronted outside a church by Justice Department agent Mitch Weaver, who reveals the extensive criminal activities of John Milton's law firm. As Weaver details the horrific truth about one of Kevin's past clients, the scene intercuts with Milton inside the church, causing holy water to boil with his touch. Suddenly, Weaver is struck and killed by a speeding car mid-sentence, leaving Kevin in shock.

setting.location: New York City street and inside a Catholic church

dialogue_highlights: He had the body of a 10-year-old girl in the trunk of his car.
Milton is into everything.

trivia.text: Hackford wanted suggestions that Milton does not kill Barzoon as he defied his muggers, or United States Attorney Weaver, who arrogantly did not watch for vehicles before stepping onto the road.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows United States Attorney Weaver stepping onto the road without looking for vehicles, resulting in him being struck by a car.

trivia.text: The outside of Central Presbyterian Church was photographed for Barzoon's funeral, while Pacino was inside the Manhattan Church of the Most Holy Redeemer for the holy water sequence.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the exterior of the church during Barzoon's funeral, as well as Pacino inside a church dipping his fingers into a holy water font.

scenes.summary: Kevin visits Mary Ann in the hospital. His mother reveals that Milton is his father. Mary Ann, hallucinating, locks herself in her room and commits suicide with a shard of glass while Kevin watches helplessly.

content_desc.clip_title: Mary Ann's Tragic Suicide in the Hospital
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin's mother confronts him in a hospital corridor to reveal a shocking truth about his father. Inside a nearby room, Mary Ann looks into a hand mirror and sees a terrifying demonic reflection. Panicking, she locks herself inside and shatters the mirror. As Kevin desperately begs her to open the door, Mary Ann uses a glass shard to take her own life.

dialogue_highlights: Your father was a waiter in that restaurant.
Behold, I send you out as sheep amidst the wolves!
Please stay with me, Mare!

trivia.text: The screenwriters added the plot element that Lomax was Milton's son, and that Milton could produce the Antichrist, neither of which are in the novel.
trivia.match_reasoning: The scene features the pivotal plot revelation where Alice confesses the truth about Kevin's parentage, establishing the added plot element that Lomax is Milton's son.

trivia.text: Additionally, Baker created images for demonic faces seen on real actresses and actors, with hands also appearing to move underneath Tamara Tunie's skin, a digital creation with the contributions of Richard Greenberg and Stephanie Powell.
trivia.match_reasoning: When Mary Ann looks into the hand mirror, the reflection of the woman behind her morphs into one of the demonic faces created by Rick Baker.

scenes.summary: Kevin confronts his mother in the hospital waiting area. He demands to know the truth about his father, and she reveals that John Milton is his father. Kevin leaves in shock and anger.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Discovers the Truth About His Father
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin, with blood on his shirt, confronts his mother in a hospital waiting area. He aggressively demands she finish a story about a man she met in New York decades ago. Tearfully, she confesses that the man was John Milton, making him Kevin's true father. Realizing Milton has been manipulating his entire life, Kevin leaves to confront him despite his mother's desperate pleas.

dialogue_highlights: Milton, he's your father.
Behold, I send you out as sheep amidst the wolves.
Playing us like a game.

trivia.text: The screenwriters added the plot element that Lomax was Milton's son, and that Milton could produce the Antichrist, neither of which are in the novel.
trivia.match_reasoning: In this scene, Alice Lomax explicitly reveals to Kevin that John Milton is his father, matching the fact about this added plot element.

scenes.summary: Kevin exits the hospital and finds Pam waiting for him. She tells him that Milton is waiting and will take away his fear. Kevin walks down an eerily empty New York street towards Milton's building.

content_desc.clip_title: Kevin Lomax Walks Down an Empty City Street
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax exits a building and encounters Pam, who is smoking outside. She notices his terror and reassures him that 'he' will take away his fear, urging him to go inside because 'he' is waiting. Kevin then turns and walks down a completely deserted New York City avenue. The surreal emptiness of the street is accompanied by an ominous Latin choir.

setting.location: Outside a corporate building and an empty New York City street

dialogue_highlights: Look at you. You're terrified.
He'll take away that fear.
He's waiting for you.

trivia.text: When Lomax leaves to meet Milton, he walks on 57th Street in New York, abnormally devoid of people or vehicles.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows Lomax walking down a New York street that is completely empty of people and vehicles after being told Milton is waiting for him.

trivia.text: It was shot on the actual 57th Street, with the filmmakers having it emptied at 7:30 a.m. on a Sunday.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features the shot of the abnormally empty 57th Street, which was cleared of traffic and pedestrians for this specific scene.

scenes.summary: Kevin enters Milton's office and confronts him about Mary Ann's death. Milton reveals his true identity as Satan and Kevin's father. Christabella appears, and Milton proposes that Kevin and his half-sister conceive the Antichrist.

content_desc.clip_title: Milton's Monologue on God, Free Will, and Vanity
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Kevin Lomax confronts John Milton, demanding to know what he did to his wife. Milton reveals that he only sets the stage and that Kevin's own vanity and choices led to her demise. As the confrontation escalates, Milton reveals his true identity as Satan and Kevin's father. He delivers a passionate monologue critiquing God and declaring himself a humanist, before proposing that Kevin and his half-sister take over the firm.

dialogue_highlights: Vanity... is definitely my favorite sin.
Let me give you a little inside information about God. God likes to watch. He's a prankster.
I'm a fan of man! I'm a humanist. Maybe the last humanist.

trivia.text: As the screenplay developed, free-will became a theme in which Milton does not actually cause events.
trivia.match_reasoning: Milton explicitly discusses the concept of free will in this scene, telling Kevin, 'Free will. It's like butterfly wings... I only set the stage. You pull your own strings.'

trivia.text: The screenwriters added the plot element that Lomax was Milton's son, and that Milton could produce the Antichrist, neither of which are in the novel.
trivia.match_reasoning: In this scene, Milton reveals that he is Kevin's father and proposes that Kevin and his half-sister Christabella conceive a child, which implies the Antichrist.

trivia.text: In an early version of the screenplay, the "better to reign in Hell than serve in Heaven" quotation is given to Milton rather than Lomax.
trivia.match_reasoning: Kevin Lomax speaks the exact line 'Better to reign in Hell than serve in Heaven' during his confrontation with Milton in this clip.

trivia.text: Shots of ballerinas moving in water were used as a basis for Milton's animated sculpture.
trivia.match_reasoning: The animated sculpture, which features moving human figures, is prominently visible on the wall behind Milton throughout the scene.

trivia.text: Williams said that he filmed the people for the statue effect, and that they were naked and placed in a tank next to a blue screen.
trivia.match_reasoning: This fact details the visual effects process used to create the animated sculpture of moving bodies clearly visible behind Milton in this scene.

trivia.text: It took three months to film the people and add the computer effects, at a cost of $2 million, which was 40% of the overall budget for special effects.
trivia.match_reasoning: This fact provides production details about the computer effects for the animated sculpture that is a central set piece in this scene.

scenes.summary: Milton reveals his grand plan for Kevin to father the Antichrist with his half-sister, Christabella. Rejecting Milton's offer, Kevin exercises his free will by shooting himself in the head, causing Milton to erupt in a fiery rage that consumes the room.
